question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance stream file containing exactly batchSize=200,000 entries per batch boundary, causing the AccountBalanceFileParser.doParse() to flush partial batches mid-transaction, leading to inconsistent account balance state persisted to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered balance file with exactly batchSize (200,000) duplicate account entries, causing the duplicate-detection logic in AccountBalanceFileParser.doParse() to silently skip entries via the putIfAbsent check, resulting in incorrect token balance state being persisted without any error signal?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted balance file whose line lengths are each close to fileBufferSize=200,000 bytes, causing CsvBalanceFileReader to allocate a 200,000-byte BufferedReader per parse invocation and exhaust JVM heap memory, resulting in an OutOfMemoryError that bypasses the @Retryable excludes=OutOfMemoryError.class guard and halts balance ingestion permanently?
